top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on flat, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic UCI Granfondo Bogotá's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before December.
68 miles on flat burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Base fitness for UCI Granfondo Bogotá. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
UCI Granfondo Bogotá's flat forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
72ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of UCI Granfondo Bogotá. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,921ft of climbing on flat does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on flat. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
At UCI Granfondo Bogotá, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 68 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for UCI Granfondo Bogotá’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: UCI Granfondo Bogotá with 4,921ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 68 miles of flat in Bogotá, Colombia.
